attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a frying pan with a fork in it on a round table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene appears to be a kitchen/dining area with two main surfaces visible: a **round white table** (foreground/top image) and a **tiled kitchen counter/island** (bottom image). No robot/agent is visible; per the task setup, the agent is **initially not at any object location**.

## Receptacles / locations (domain-relevant)
- **Round table** (receptacle / surface)
  - A white, circular table with a wooden chair partly visible on the left side.
  - Multiple small items are resting on top of it (described below).
  - This is the **target location** mentioned in the instruction (“on a round table”).
- **Kitchen counter / island** (receptacle / surface)
  - Beige tiled countertop with wooden cabinets underneath.
  - At least two objects are on it (bread-like item and a spatula-like utensil).

No **microwave**, **fridge**, or **sink** is clearly visible in these images, and no openable receptacles (doors/drawers) are evident.

## Objects visible on the round table (top image)
- **Fork-like utensil** (not a defined domain type; treated as a generic object)
  - A metal utensil with a fork head is visible near the right side of the round table, close to two green oblong items (vegetable-like).
  - It appears to be lying flat on the table surface.
- **Knife-like object** (could match domain type `knife` if the system treats it as such; visually it is a long thin utensil/blade)
  - A long, thin object lies toward the left side of the table; it resembles a knife or skewer.
- **Paper towel roll**
  - A white vertical roll standing near the center of the table.
- **Green oblong items (2)**
  - Two green, cucumber/zucchini-like objects near the right side of the table, next to the fork.
- **Brown oval item**
  - A potato-like brown object near the lower center-left of the table.
- **Dark green bottle**
  - A bottle (possibly wine/olive oil) near the lower right area of the table.
- **Small green box/carton**
  - A small green rectangular container near the upper left/center of the table.
- **Small round brown item**
  - An egg-like object near the upper right edge of the table.

## Objects visible on the kitchen counter (bottom image)
- **Loaf-like brown object**
  - On the left side of the countertop; looks like a loaf of bread.
- **Spatula/turner utensil**
  - On the right side of the countertop; a flat metal head with a dark handle.

## Key missing/uncertain items for the instruction
- **Frying pan**: not clearly visible on either the round table or the counter in the provided images.
- **Fork**: a fork is visible on the round table, but it is **not inside a frying pan** (no pan is seen).

## Spatial relationships (predicate-oriented, from what is visible)
- Many objects are **on the round table surface** (so they are effectively *in/on* that receptacle location).
  - The fork is on the round table, close to the two green oblong items.
  - The bottle is on the round table near the lower right.
  - The potato-like item is on the round table near the lower center-left.
  - The paper towel roll is near the center of the round table.
- On the kitchen counter, the loaf-like object is on the left; the spatula is on the right.

## Current relevant states
- No object is shown being held by the agent (agent not visible), so **nothing is currently held**.
- No evidence of objects being **clean**, **hot**, **cool**, **sliced**, **toggled/on**, or any receptacle being **opened**.

## Summary for the task goal (“Put a frying pan with a fork in it on a round table”)
- The **round table** is present and accessible as the destination receptacle.
- A **fork** is present **on the round table**, but **no frying pan is visible**, and the fork is not contained in a pan in the current view.
- The frying pan (and possibly another fork if required) likely exists outside the visible area or is occluded.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
(define (problem put_frying_pan_with_fork_on_round_table)
  (:domain put_task)

  (:objects
    agent1 - agent

    round_table - receptacle
    kitchen_counter - receptacle

    frying_pan - receptacle
    fork - object

    paper_towel_roll - object
    green_veg1 - object
    green_veg2 - object
    potato - object
    bottle - object
    green_box - object
    egg - object
    loaf - object
    spatula - object

    knife1 - knife
  )

  (:init
    ;; agent starts not at any object's location (no atLocation facts for agent1)

    ;; receptacles/surfaces in the scene
    ;; (round_table and kitchen_counter are receptacles by type)

    ;; objects on/in the round table (modeled with inReceptacle)
    (inReceptacle fork round_table)
    (inReceptacle paper_towel_roll round_table)
    (inReceptacle green_veg1 round_table)
    (inReceptacle green_veg2 round_table)
    (inReceptacle potato round_table)
    (inReceptacle bottle round_table)
    (inReceptacle green_box round_table)
    (inReceptacle egg round_table)
    (inReceptacle knife1 round_table)

    ;; objects on/in the kitchen counter
    (inReceptacle loaf kitchen_counter)
    (inReceptacle spatula kitchen_counter)

    ;; frying pan is not visible in the provided view; include it as an object in the scene,
    ;; initially on the kitchen counter (a plausible starting location distinct from the goal).
    (inReceptacle frying_pan kitchen_counter)
  )

  (:goal
    (and
      ;; frying pan is on the round table
      (inReceptacle frying_pan round_table)
      ;; fork is in the frying pan
      (inReceptacle fork frying_pan)
    )
  )
)

attempts.plan:
(gotolocation agent1 round_table)
(pickupobject agent1 fork)
(gotolocation agent1 frying_pan)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 fork frying_pan)
(pickupobject agent1 frying_pan)
(gotolocation agent1 round_table)
(putobjectinreceptacle agent1 frying_pan round_table)
